sweep_metadata.span_note: First sweep since Day 119 (June 25); covers the June 26-29 span against the v4.5 anchor (T9 CONTESTED enacted; A4 structurally-opaque; A2 inherited). The cycle's defining mover is a REAL bilateral US-Iran kinetic exchange that fired then self-corrected: (1) June 25 the Singapore-flagged container ship Ever Lovely was drone-attacked off Oman (Trump: 4 Iranian drones, 3 shot down); (2) June 26-27 CENTCOM struck FIVE Iranian coastal sites (surveillance/comms/air-defense/drone-storage/mine-laying; Qeshm radar; two newly-built per JPost) on a self-defense-of-shipping framing, NO operation name; June 27 the Panama-flagged tanker Kiku was drone-struck; (3) June 28 (2-3am local) IRGC naval+aerospace launched ballistic missiles+drones at Ali Al Salem AB (Kuwait) and the US Fifth Fleet HQ Port Salman (Bahrain), claiming '8 US bases destroyed' -- CENTCOM/US official (Reuters) report ZERO damage, ZERO US casualties; (4) late June 28 Washington-Tehran agreed to cease and resume Doha talks Tuesday June 30 (Trump confirmed; Iran did NOT confirm -- Gharibabadi: technical talks 'not yet planned,' only 'when conditions are met'). Separately: Israel-Lebanon SIGNED a framework June 26 (ties Israeli withdrawal to Hezbollah disarmament; Hezbollah chief calls it 'null and void'); IAEA DG Grossi asserted the MoU 'explicitly' mandates IAEA supervision while Iran (Gharibabadi) defers access to a final agreement (P109-03 continues); Brent rose ~0.9% to ~$73.21 on the strikes (war premium nearly fully unwound). Source quality high this cycle: the strike exchange, the base strikes, the vessel names/flags, the Gulf condemnations, and the oil tape are all multi-outlet T2 (Al Jazeera, NPR, CNN, CBS, PBS, CBC, Fox, ABC, JPost). Standard 2025-12-Day-War web contamination present; tier discounts applied per card.

reference_trends.note: Mosaic-Octopus multi-channel extended deterrent USE-CONFIRMED again and calibrated below the maximum-response threshold. Iran exercised multiple channels in 72 hours: one-way drones on commercial shipping (Ever Lovely, Kiku) and a coordinated IRGC naval+aerospace ballistic-missile+drone strike on US bases in TWO Gulf states (Ali Al Salem AB Kuwait; 5th Fleet HQ Bahrain). The demonstration was calibrated: pre-dawn timing, IRGC claims '8 bases destroyed' while CENTCOM reports ZERO damage and ZERO US KIA, immediately followed by a de-escalation agreement to resume Doha talks. Loss/threat to one channel did not collapse the network; apex publicly reserved nothing while the military brandished. Textbook Slantchev calibrated-below-maximum demonstration. Advance within VALIDATED

Powell shifting-power Israeli pre-emption: incentive remains maximal (440kg HEU retained, verification disputed) but UNSPENT on an Iran-nuclear strike even during an active US-Iran kinetic exchange -- the strongest non-spend datapoint yet. The reserved Lebanon axis de-escalated at the state level: Israel-Lebanon SIGNED a framework June 26 (Israeli withdrawal tied to Hezbollah disarmament). Hezbollah chief calls it 'null and void,' keeping the spoiler latent. Ben Gvir/Smotrich reject the Iran MoU ('does not bind us'); no far-right resignation. Hold/easing within VALIDATED

Stage-2 hysteresis lock-in, CONTESTED (enacted v4.5). Executive-path prediction REINFORCED this cycle: (a) backfill -- one day after the 50-48 passage the Senate REJECTED a measure restricting Trump's Iran war powers 50-47 (procedural; only 2 R for), a countervailing on-merits datapoint; (b) decisively, Trump ORDERED CENTCOM strikes on five Iranian sites June 26-27, days after both chambers passed the WPR and he dismissed it -- the executive struck Iran with zero enforceable constraint, the operational lock-in holding in fact. No new WPR vote, no court standing. Holds CONTESTED (not back to VALIDATED -- the bicameral on-merits passages stand; not to DISCONFIRMED -- executive path operational). No state change

Tripolar unaligned-middle: Gulf autonomous pivot capacity holds; disposition toward IRAN hardened sharply because Iran struck Gulf soil. Unified condemnation: Bahrain ('violated sovereignty,' undermined de-escalation), Kuwait ('repeated heinous Iranian aggressions'), UAE ('blatant violation' of sovereignty), Jordan ('dangerous escalation'). This is convergent Gulf condemnation of Iran, NOT a named MBS-MBZ split (UAE aligned with Bahrain/Kuwait). Distinct from the brake-against-US-action function (which concerns restraining US/Israeli strikes ON Iran); here the unaligned middle exercises autonomous voice AGAINST Iranian aggression on its territory. Holds within VALIDATED

Fearon-Slantchev apex-deterrent floor / two-level. Apex structurally-opaque (A4, v4.5): the base strikes were IRGC-attributed (naval+aerospace command) with NO apex/SNSC ownership claim, and the rapid cease-and-resume-Doha agreement points to council-level control of the off-ramp -- consistent with a diffuse/delegated council that consents to the deal while the military brandishes the floor. No authenticated apex-direct statement (Mojtaba unseen since Feb 28; P115-03 unmet; P84-07 Vahidi-HEU absent, 12th). A4 resurrection condition did NOT fire. Holds within VALIDATED

US eschatological-coalition disaggregation: deal-faction (Trump+Vance) operationally dominant -- struck on a self-defense-of-shipping framing limited to military infrastructure, then re-opened Doha; Vance 'violence will be met with violence' + 'pick up the phone.' No eschatological/maximalist counter-mobilization, no nuclear-site or principal targeting. Holds within VALIDATED

Reconstitution-speed Powell amplifier: no fresh reconstitution cluster this cycle; the standing US-IC/Israeli-IC baseline carries (faster-than-IC timelines; ~half launchers intact; ~440kg 60% HEU survived). Capability frozen-pending-verification at the table. Hold within VALIDATED

calibration.trend_state_question: NO state transition warranted this cycle. T9 just transitioned to CONTESTED at v4.5 and HOLDS CONTESTED (executive path reinforced -- Trump struck Iran days after the WPR; the June-24 Senate procedural rejection 50-47 countervails; neither return-to-VALIDATED nor advance-to-DISCONFIRMED is met). T2 advances within VALIDATED (multi-channel deterrent use-confirmed, calibrated below maximum). T1/T3/T4/T8/T12 hold within state. No VALIDATED trend contradicted. DISCIPLINE ESCALATION (BS-7/BS-11 Hormuz sub-read; NOW A METHODOLOGY FINDING, not just a watch item): the Hormuz/shipping operational sub-read has reversed FOUR consecutive cycles -- D110 'settled/traded-in-text' -> D115 'contested-leverage re-arm' (Fork C up) -> D119 'declaratory/unenforced, de-escalating' (Fork C down to 14-22) -> D123 'live kinetic exchange' (US+Iran actual strikes; Fork C back up). The Day-119 sweep flagged its own de-arm as a /premortem watch item ('is the Hormuz sub-read tracking each headline?'); THIS CYCLE CONFIRMS IT. The Day-119 Fork-C cut to 14-22 on one quiet cycle was recency-leaning in the de-escalation direction, mirroring the Day-115 over-read in the escalation direction. The disciplined read: the shipping/Hormuz surface is a PERSISTENT LIVE accident surface that should not be re-cut sharply each cycle on the latest headline. Route to /audit (BS-7/BS-11 state note + a standing-Fork-C-floor recommendation so the surface is not zeroed on quiet cycles) and to /premortem (Hormuz-sub-read headline-tracking, now a 4-cycle confirmed pattern). This is logged as a within-architecture recency-bias process finding, NOT a trend contradiction (no VALIDATED trend predicted Hormuz-term permanence; the surface is a residual per §5.28/§5.30).
calibration.thesis_question: HOLDING -- and stress-tested harder this cycle than at any point since the signing. The v4.5 thin-instrument-defers-collisions thesis absorbed a REAL bilateral kinetic exchange: the US struck five Iranian sites and Iran struck US bases in two Gulf states, and the constraint surface still compressed both principals back toward Doha within ~48 hours with ZERO US KIA, no new US operation, and a mutual cease agreement. The deferral did not break under live fire; it bent and reset. The §5.30 verification collision continues exactly where ranked (Grossi vs Iran on IAEA access). The materialist reading holds: neither principal could afford the cascade-to-Fork-A (US -- no KIA to convert, deal-protective executive; Iran -- diffuse council wants the sanctions relief), so each selected the off-ramp the constraint surface made cheapest. The one process concern (Hormuz sub-read oscillation) is recency bias in the framework's own operational layer, not a thesis break. The binding question stays inside the 60-day window: (a) does Iran actually show at Doha June 30 (Gharibabadi hedged), and (b) does the verification fracture (D'-v driver) harden. The kinetic surfaces proved LIVE, not declaratory -- the correction the framework owes the Day-119 read.

Fork_C.rationale: RE-ARM and FIRE. The accident surface the Day-119 sweep cut to 14-22 on a 'declaratory/unenforced' read produced an actual two-way kinetic exchange within days (Ever Lovely + Kiku drone attacks -> CENTCOM strikes on 5 Iranian sites -> IRGC strikes on US bases in Kuwait/Bahrain). The surface is demonstrably LIVE, not declaratory. It de-escalated (cease + Doha June 30), so this is a Fork C realization that self-corrected, not a Fork A breakout -- hence up to 18-28, not higher. The 10pp width reflects genuine boundary uncertainty (the surface fired but reset); not a hedge

Fork_D_prime_composite.direction: down (mass to Fork C; internal tilt to D'-v)
Fork_D_prime_composite.rationale: The signed deferral SURVIVED a live kinetic exchange (talks set to resume) -- strong thesis confirmation -- but the window is no longer clean: actual strikes occurred, Iran did NOT confirm Doha participation (Gharibabadi: 'when conditions are met'), and the verification fracture continues. Composite down ~2pp; the clean-operative variant takes the hit

Fork_D_prime_i_operative.rationale: The clean-operative deferral path degraded: the 60-day window absorbed a bilateral strike exchange and Iran is hedging Doha attendance. The path holds (cease agreement; talks scheduled) but is no longer 'clean operative'

Fork_D_prime_v_signed_but_stalls.rationale: The verification fracture persists and firms qualitatively: Grossi now asserts the MoU 'explicitly' mandates IAEA supervision while Iran defers access to a final agreement -- a continuing principal-level contradiction (P109-03). The kinetic exchange is an additional stall-stress, but the instrument held (signature intact, talks scheduled), so the band holds rather than widening

Fork_A_composite.rationale: HELD under live fire. The US struck Iran and Iran struck US bases, and it did NOT become Fork A: zero US KIA, no new sustained operation, mutual de-escalation, talks resumed -- the cascade-to-A barrier held. US-resumption sub-path ticks up within band (Trump demonstrated strike-willingness; 'Iran will no longer exist' / 'complete the job' rhetoric; next vessel incident could re-trigger) but is offset by the Israeli pre-emption sub-path easing (Lebanon framework signed). Israeli pre-emption sub-path holds 28-40% within window; US Vahidi-decapitation standalone holds 4-10% (no principal targeting; strikes hit infrastructure not principals)

Fork_B_combined.rationale: The 60-day roadmap and the survival of the deferral through a live exchange keep the comprehensive-settlement path alive; the IAEA-verification fracture and Iran's Doha hedging cap it below D'. Hold

KEC_derived.note: [DERIVED] Fork A 10-18% + Fork C 18-28% + tail (<2%). Footnote use only; primitives lead. Up-tick tracks the Fork C re-arm as the shipping/Hormuz surface fired into a live exchange

calibration.note_6_12m_matrix: No 6/12m structural-prior update from the sweep. The June-26-28 kinetic exchange is an annex-level activation of the v4.5 §5.28/§5.30 residual surfaces (accident-surface firing then de-escalating), not a constraint-layer shift. No trend-state transition this cycle (T9 already CONTESTED at v4.5; holds). The structural priors resting on T2/T8/T12 are unaffected. A standing /revise watch carries from v4.5: a Federal court granting WPA standing OR a veto-override-capable majority would move T9 toward DISCONFIRMED and force the Fork A 6/12m re-base; neither occurred (the executive struck Iran free of constraint, the opposite direction).

finding_cards.target: Energy market structural breaks (BS-7) -- Brent, Hormuz status, closure/reopening, war-risk pricing
sources_consulted: Al Jazeera ('Oil prices rise as US, Iranian strikes threaten Strait of Hormuz reopening', June 29)
Al Jazeera (June 17, oil slide on peace/reopening)
World Bank Blogs (Hormuz disruption / oil)
TradingEconomics (Brent ~$73.21, Aug futures)
JMIC/US Navy (widened Oman-side route, June 27)
discounts_applied: Tracker/aggregator route-detail items held at T4 pending T1/T2 confirmation
IRGC '8 bases destroyed' damage claim excluded from the oil read (CENTCOM zero-damage adjudication, see PROBE-2/PROBE-7)
finding_cards.finding: The war-risk premium ticked UP modestly rather than collapsing: Brent rose ~0.9% to ~$73.21/bbl (Aug futures) on June 29 after the weekend US-Iran strike exchange renewed doubts about normal Hormuz shipping. The level is still only ~$0.13 above the Feb-28 pre-war price -- an analyst noted oil had 'nearly unwound its entire war premium, despite an MoU with no enforcement details and ongoing strikes.' On June 27 the US-Navy-overseen JMIC announced a WIDENED route through the strait near Oman (both-directions naval traffic), challenging Iranian control. This CORRECTS the Day-119 'declaratory/unenforced, de-escalating' read: the shipping/Hormuz surface proved LIVE -- two commercial vessels (Ever Lovely, Kiku) were drone-attacked, drawing actual US strikes. P108-04 (Brent >$92) and P105-05 (Brent >$100 sustained) did-NOT-fire decisively.
finding_cards.trigger_match: yes -- BS-7 re-arm: the shipping/Hormuz accident surface fired into a real exchange (vessels hit; US strikes; Iran base strikes) but the oil tape priced only a modest risk re-add (~$73), not a closure premium. Drives the Fork C re-arm (14-22 -> 18-28). P108-04/P105-05 resolve did-not-fire
finding_cards.direction: up (Fork C / war-risk surface live again); oil level itself near-flat (~$73), war premium nearly fully unwound
finding_cards.proposed_revision: BS-7/BS-11 state note: the Hormuz/shipping surface reverts AGAIN to LIVE-kinetic (FOURTH consecutive oscillation: D110 settled -> D115 re-arm -> D119 declaratory/de-arm -> D123 live-exchange). Matrix: Fork C 14-22 -> 18-28. Resolve P108-04/P105-05 did-not-fire. ESCALATE the oscillation to /audit (recommend a standing Fork-C floor so the surface is not zeroed on quiet cycles) and /premortem (confirmed 4-cycle headline-tracking pattern)
reference_trends: T2 advance -- shipping channel exercised as live leverage (vessel attacks) then capped (de-escalation), calibrated below the closure/oil-spike threshold
T9 hold -- Hormuz regime traded in the executive instrument; the dispute runs inside it
finding_cards.conflict_notes: The oil tape (T2 multi-source) is the authoritative read over both the IRGC closure/strike claims and any single tracker. The near-flat ~$73 level despite a live strike exchange is itself the signal: the market prices the de-escalation/Doha resumption over the kinetic headline. Recency-bias self-correction: the Day-119 Fork-C cut to 14-22 on the quiet June-20-25 window was de-escalation-leaning; this cycle's re-arm is the mirror-image correction. The disciplined position is a persistent live surface, not a per-cycle re-cut.

finding_cards.target: CENTCOM posture / residual capability (BS-13) -- two-axis nominal/operational score, transit-enforcement, US strikes on Iran
sources_consulted: NPR ('US strikes Iran', June 27)', 'CENTCOM via reporting (5 Iranian coastal sites; surveillance/comms/air-defense/drone-storage/mine-laying; Qeshm radar)
Jerusalem Post (two CENTCOM targets newly-built by Iran)
Reuters via reporting (US official: no US casualties/damage)
Al Jazeera (June 28-29)
discounts_applied: Trump 'Iran will no longer exist' / 'complete the job' rhetoric scored near-zero on literal content; the strikes are the tape action
IRGC base-damage claims excluded (CENTCOM zero-damage adjudication)
finding_cards.finding: CENTCOM CONVERTED to the operational-escalation axis this cycle: under Trump's direction, US aircraft struck FIVE Iranian coastal/military sites June 26-27 (surveillance infrastructure, communications, air defense, drone storage, mine-laying capability; Qeshm Island radar), framed as a direct self-defense response to Iranian attacks on commercial shipping (Ever Lovely June 25; Kiku June 27). Two targets were newly-built Iranian sites (JPost). CRITICALLY, the operational axis stayed BOUNDED: no new operation name (P105-01 did-not-fire), no principal or nuclear-site targeting, infrastructure-only, and ZERO US KIA from Iran's retaliatory base strikes (US official to Reuters: no casualties/damage). The two-axis read this cycle: nominal = self-defense/reactive framing (Iran-violated-the-ceasefire); operational = MODERATE escalation (actual strikes on Iranian territory, but infrastructure-scoped and immediately followed by a de-escalation agreement). This is the 'pressure-while-talking' middle register the recalibrated PROBE-7 was built to score -- escalated higher than D119 (which was clean de-escalation) but well short of the D105 peak or a Fork A breakout.
finding_cards.trigger_match: yes -- US resumed kinetic strikes on Iranian soil (operational-axis escalation) on a self-defense framing (nominal-axis reactive), bounded to infrastructure with zero US KIA and a mutual cease. Feeds the Fork C re-arm; the Fork A activation watch (new sustained operation OR US KIA) UNFIRED. P105-01 did-not-fire; P93-04 (US KIA) did-not-fire
finding_cards.direction: up (operational axis -- actual strikes on Iran), bounded (no operation name, no KIA, no principal/nuclear targeting, de-escalation)
finding_cards.proposed_revision: PROBE-7 two-axis score reads MIDDLE-register escalation-then-de-escalation this cycle (vs D119 clean de-escalation, D105 escalation peak) -- the recalibration tracks the full arc without a false read. BS-13 state note: US demonstrated willingness to strike Iranian military infrastructure under a self-defense-of-shipping framing while protecting the deal track (struck, then re-opened Doha). Folds into the PROBE-8/PROBE-16 Fork C re-arm. Watch: any NEXT strike that names an operation, hits a principal/nuclear site, or produces US KIA converts to a Fork A trigger
reference_trends: T4 hold -- deal-faction executive struck on a bounded self-defense framing then re-opened talks
T9 hold -- the executive struck Iran days after the WPR passed, lock-in operational
finding_cards.conflict_notes: The bounded character is the load-bearing finding: maximal rhetoric ('Iran will no longer exist') paired with infrastructure-only strikes, zero principal targeting, and an immediate de-escalation agreement = deal-protective coercion, NOT a Fork A entry. The discriminating test passed in the de-escalation direction. No principal-validation issue (CENTCOM/Trump attribution named, on-tape).

finding_cards.target: IRGC factional structure (BS-8) + Iranian decision architecture (BS-1a) -- Khatam al-Anbiya / IRGC vertices, strike authority, HEU statements
sources_consulted: Al Jazeera ('Iran attacks Kuwait and Bahrain', June 28)
PBS NewsHour (Iran attacks Bahrain/Kuwait, threatens to halt talks)
CBC ('Iran-US exchange attacks Sunday')
IRGC statement (named, '8 US infrastructures destroyed'; naval+aerospace; Ali Al Salem / Port Salman)
US official via Reuters (zero damage/casualties)
discounts_applied: -50% on classified internal-decision content (who ordered the base strikes)
IRGC '8 bases destroyed' read for posture, not literal effect (CENTCOM zero-damage)
Iran state-military statements read for signaling, not literal intent
finding_cards.finding: The IRGC executed the cycle's deterrent response as a COORDINATED naval+aerospace operation: ballistic missiles + drones on the Ali Al Salem Air Base (Kuwait) and the US Fifth Fleet HQ at Port Salman (Bahrain), 2-3am local June 28, claiming '8 US military infrastructures destroyed' as retaliation for the US strikes on five Iranian coastal sites. CENTCOM/US official: ZERO damage, ZERO casualties -- the claim-vs-effect gap marks a CALIBRATED demonstration (face-saving maximal claim; sub-threshold actual effect). The operation was IRGC-attributed with NO apex/SNSC ownership claim, and the immediate cease-and-resume-Doha agreement points to council-level control of the off-ramp. Vahidi-direct HEU statement again ABSENT (P84-07, 12th consecutive cycle). The civil-military pattern from D119 (IRGC brandishes; diplomatic apparatus negotiates) recurs: the IRGC struck, the deal track (Doha June 30) re-opened within hours.
finding_cards.trigger_match: yes (factional-signal + multi-channel deterrent) -- IRGC executed a coordinated, calibrated, sub-threshold base strike on two Gulf states; no apex fingerprint; immediately capped by a de-escalation agreement. Feeds the Fork C realization (live exchange) and the T2 advance (calibrated demonstration). No HEU-axis movement
finding_cards.direction: up then capped (Hormuz-axis kinetic risk fired but de-escalated; the strike was demonstrative/calibrated, zero US KIA)
finding_cards.proposed_revision: BS-8 state note: the IRGC naval+aerospace command is the active deterrent vertex; it executed a two-country base strike calibrated below the maximum-response threshold (zero effect, immediate de-escalation) with no apex ownership claim -- the escalation-control reading tilts toward coordinated/calibrated signaling under a structurally-opaque council, NOT uncontrolled IRGC autonomy. Carry to /audit
reference_trends: T2 advance -- multi-channel deterrent use-confirmed, calibrated below maximum (claim-vs-effect gap; immediate de-escalation)
T3 hold -- military brandishes/exercises the floor; the diffuse council consents to the deal (Doha re-opens); apex does not own the strike
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Principal-validation (A4 STRUCTURALLY-OPAQUE per v4.5; window closed, no provisional forcing-question owed). The escalation-control discriminator (apex/council-directed base strikes vs IRGC-autonomous) remains unresolved but the evidence tilts toward COORDINATED-CALIBRATED: the strike was demonstrative (pre-dawn, zero-effect, maximal-claim) and immediately followed by a council-consistent de-escalation to talks -- a calibrated signal under a diffuse council, not a runaway military. No apex claim; A4 resurrection condition (authenticated apex-direct statement) did not fire; any new attribution enters at provisional confidence. Adjudication of the '8 bases destroyed' vs 'zero damage' conflict: CENTCOM/US-official (T2) on damage over IRGC posture (-50%).

finding_cards.target: First-mover architecture under asymmetric lock-in (BS-15) -- accident-surface / snap-back race
sources_consulted: NPR (US/Iran retaliatory strikes)
Al Jazeera (June 28-29 exchange)
CBS/CNN live (ceasefire strained)
Reuters via reporting (zero US KIA)
finding_cards.finding: The accident surface FIRED and then self-corrected -- the cleanest Fork C realization of the conflict to date. Surface 1 (Hormuz/shipping) produced an actual race: Iranian drone attacks on two commercial vessels -> US strikes on five Iranian sites -> Iranian missile/drone strikes on US bases in Kuwait/Bahrain -> mutual cease + Doha-resumption agreement within ~48 hours. The race ran its first full loop and de-escalated: the Fork C-to-Fork A conversion did NOT occur because the convertible triggers (US KIA, sustained operation, principal/nuclear targeting) all stayed unmet, and both principals selected the off-ramp. Surface 2 (Lebanon) de-escalated at the state level (framework signed June 26; Hezbollah rejects). Surfaces 3 (Iran-Israel direct) and 4 (Iran-GCC) -- note Surface 4 partially activated (Iran struck Gulf-hosted US bases) but drew condemnation, not a Gulf-Iran war. The joint accident probability is HIGHER than the D119 cooled read (the surface demonstrably fires) but the de-escalation mechanism is also demonstrated robust.
finding_cards.trigger_match: yes -- the accident surface fired into a live US-Iran exchange (Fork C realization) and self-corrected without converting to Fork A. The Fork C / Fork A boundary held under live fire (no KIA, no operation, de-escalation)
finding_cards.direction: up (Fork C realized live), bounded (no Fork A conversion; de-escalation mechanism held)
finding_cards.proposed_revision: No standalone matrix move beyond the PROBE-8/PROBE-7/PROBE-2 Fork C re-arm. BS-15 state note: the first-mover race ran its first full loop (incident -> strike -> counter-strike -> de-escalation) and the cascade-to-A barrier HELD -- material evidence that the de-escalation mechanism is robust even under a real bilateral exchange. Flag for /audit: the surface is structurally live; the Fork C floor should reflect that (see PROBE-8 oscillation note)
reference_trends: T2 advance -- the race ran and capped below maximum
T8 hold/easing -- Lebanon surface to a signed framework; no Israeli piggyback strike on Iran during the exchange
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Joint-distribution opacity at the Fork C/Fork A boundary (BS-15 known limitation) was directly TESTED this cycle and resolved in the de-escalation direction: a real exchange did not convert. Principal-validation note (mirrors PROBE-2): the base strikes were IRGC-attributed and apex-unowned, calibrated and immediately de-escalated -- low apex escalation-intent despite the live exchange. The D119-noted single-surface cooling is reversed (the surface fired); the cascade-conversion concern is partially eased (the barrier held).

finding_cards.target: Diplomacy track (BS-11) -- talks progress, collision classes (HEU/IAEA verification), Doha resumption, signatories, channels
sources_consulted: Al Jazeera ('UN nuclear chief says inspections will happen, Tehran says after deal', June 24)
NPR (Grossi: inspectors will visit, Iran says only after final deal, June 24)
CNBC (US-Iran deal grants access, UN watchdog says, June 26)
NPR ('Trump says US and Iran will meet in Qatar after weekend attacks', June 29)
Fox News Digital (Iran agrees to halt attacks, talks loom, June 28)
CBS (talks timetable unclear)
discounts_applied: Trump 'talks resume Tuesday' claim adjudicated against Iran's non-confirmation (Gharibabadi: 'not yet planned... when conditions are met') -- reported as a conflict, not averaged
Grossi statements T2-direct; Iranian MFA statements T1-direct
finding_cards.finding: Two diplomacy movements. (1) VERIFICATION FRACTURE firms: IAEA DG Grossi asserted the MoU 'explicitly' states that nuclear material/facilities 'will be supervised by the IAEA' and that inspections WILL happen (dates/locations being discussed); Iran's deputy FM Gharibabadi countered that inspector access 'will solely be examined and resolved within the framework of a final agreement.' The principal-level US/IAEA-vs-Iran contradiction (P109-03) thus continues, with Grossi now placing the TEXT on the supervision side -- a qualitative firming of the §5.30 collision. (2) DEFERRAL SURVIVED THE EXCHANGE: after the weekend strikes, Trump said talks resume in Doha Tuesday June 30; Iran did NOT confirm (Gharibabadi: technical talks 'not yet planned this week... when the conditions are met'). The only direct meeting since the MoU remains June 21 (Switzerland). The instrument held (signature intact; mediator Qatar active) but Iran is hedging attendance and the verification term is unresolved.
finding_cards.trigger_match: yes -- P109-03 (HEU/IAEA verification dispute) CONTINUES and firms (Grossi: text mandates supervision; Iran: access only at final agreement). The deferral survived a live kinetic exchange (talks scheduled, signature intact) but Iran's Doha attendance is unconfirmed -- a D'-i degrade. Holds D'-v as the leading variant
finding_cards.direction: D'-i down (Iran hedging Doha; window non-clean), D'-v hold (verification fracture firms but instrument holds)
finding_cards.proposed_revision: Log P109-03 continues/firms (Grossi text-assertion vs Iran final-agreement deferral). Matrix: D'-i 30-42 -> 26-38; D'-v hold 22-30; composite 50-64 -> 48-62. BS-11 state note: the signed deferral absorbed a bilateral strike exchange and held; verification (IAEA access timing) is the central disputed term, now with Grossi placing the MoU text on the supervision side. Discriminating signal next cycle: (a) does Iran actually appear at Doha June 30, (b) an agreed/published IAEA-access protocol vs sustained Iranian deferral hardening to deal-terminal
reference_trends: T4 hold -- deal-faction (Vance) leading; Grossi/IAEA channel active
T3 hold -- Iranian side defers verification to a final-agreement stage (two-level managed; apex structurally-opaque)
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Two live conflicts reported, not averaged: (1) Grossi (text mandates IAEA supervision) vs Gharibabadi (access only at final agreement) -- Grossi has the stronger claim on the TEXT (he is party to the MoU language) but Iran controls actual access; unresolved, the binding D'-v driver. (2) Trump (talks resume June 30) vs Iran non-confirmation (Gharibabadi 'when conditions met') -- the actual data is the mutual cease-fire-attacks agreement; Doha attendance is genuinely uncertain. The Vance-vs-Iran and Grossi-vs-Iran contradictions are two-level-game evidence: each side narrates the thin instrument to its own ratification audience.

finding_cards.target: A1 Trump principal disposition (BS-12) -- statement-to-action gap, deal vs coercive rhetoric, tape action
sources_consulted: NPR ('US strikes Iran', June 27)
PBS/NPR (Vance 'violence will be met with violence'; 'pick up the phone')
CNN live (Trump: Ever Lovely 'took a shot... four of them')
ABC/Fox (Trump: Iran 'will no longer exist' / 'complete the job')
NPR (Trump: talks resume Tuesday, June 29)
discounts_applied: Trump maximalist rhetoric ('Iran will no longer exist', 'complete the job') scored near-zero on literal content; the data is the STRIKE order (tape action) and the deal-protective sequencing
finding_cards.finding: A1 CONVERTED rhetoric to tape action this cycle -- the discriminating shift from Day 119 (where rhetoric stayed rhetoric). Trump ordered actual CENTCOM strikes on five Iranian sites (June 26-27) after citing the Ever Lovely drone attack as a ceasefire violation ('I don't like the fact that they took a shot yesterday, actually four of them'). Vance: 'violence will be met with violence'; Iran should 'pick up the phone.' Trump escalated rhetoric sharply ('will no longer exist' / 'complete the job'). BUT the action stayed DEAL-PROTECTIVE: the strikes were infrastructure-scoped self-defense (no principal/nuclear targeting), and Trump simultaneously announced talks resume in Doha June 30. A1 reads 'hawkish-WITH-execution, deal-protective': the statement-to-action gap CLOSED on the strike, but the strike was bounded and paired with re-opened negotiations.
finding_cards.trigger_match: yes (sub-state shift) -- A1 moved from 'hawkish-no-execution' (D119) to 'hawkish-with-bounded-execution, deal-protective' (D123): Trump ordered real strikes (tape action) but kept them infrastructure-scoped and re-opened Doha. The US-resumption Fork A sub-path ticks up within band (strike-willingness demonstrated) but is bounded (no operation name, no KIA, talks resumed). No fork-level move
finding_cards.direction: no change (Fork A composite); US-resumption sub-path ticks up within band; A1 statement-to-action gap CLOSED on the strike but bounded
finding_cards.proposed_revision: BS-12 state note: A1 converted to bounded tape action this cycle (5-site self-defense strikes) while protecting the deal (re-opened Doha) -- the deal-protective-coercion sub-state now has a use-confirmation (struck, then talked), not just compressed-but-unconverted rhetoric. Watch: a NEXT strike naming an operation / hitting a principal or nuclear site / producing US KIA converts to a Fork A trigger
reference_trends: T4 hold -- deal-faction executive using bounded coercion to protect the deal track (struck then re-opened Doha)
T3 hold -- US-side public maximalism on the end-state while protecting the deferral mechanism
finding_cards.conflict_notes: No principal-validation issue (A1 attribution to Trump named, on-tape, multi-outlet). The cycle's discriminating test: did the strike order break the deal-protective read? It did not -- the strikes were bounded (infrastructure, no KIA, no operation name) and immediately paired with a Doha resumption, confirming deal-protective coercion rather than a Fork A decision. The 'Iran will no longer exist' rhetoric is the discounted register; the bounded strike + re-opened talks is the data.

finding_cards.target: Israeli internal politics / operational posture (BS-3) -- Lebanon axis, Hezbollah, Trump-Netanyahu divergence, Iran-strike posture
sources_consulted: Al Jazeera ('Israel-Lebanon deal ties ceasefire to Hezbollah disarmament', June 27)
Al Jazeera (framework signed; sequenced process; no mandated Israeli withdrawal)
Wikipedia '2026 Israel-Lebanon peace talks' (T4, framework signed June 26)
Times of Israel (Ben Gvir/Smotrich reject Iran MoU)
discounts_applied: Hezbollah 'null and void' statement read for posture
Israel-Lebanon framework terms held at T2-Al Jazeera; the four-days-of-Washington-talks framing corroborated multi-source
finding_cards.finding: The Lebanon spoiler DE-ESCALATED at the state level: Israel and Lebanon SIGNED a framework agreement June 26 after four days of marathon US-brokered talks in Washington. It sets a 'sequenced process' for the Lebanese army to restore sovereign authority 'pending the verified disarmament of non-state armed groups' (Hezbollah), but does NOT mandate Israeli withdrawal from the ~fifth of Lebanese territory it occupies. Hezbollah's chief called the deal 'null and void,' rejecting the withdrawal-tied-to-disarmament condition -- keeping the spoiler latent. CRITICALLY, throughout the active US-Iran kinetic exchange, Israel did NOT strike Iranian nuclear sites and did not draw a direct Iranian response: the pre-emption incentive stayed unspent even with the US already striking Iran. Ben Gvir/Smotrich reject the Iran MoU ('does not bind us'); no far-right resignation; coalition strained, intact.
finding_cards.trigger_match: partial -- no Israeli strike on Iranian nuclear sites (P85-02 unfired); no direct Iran-Israel exchange (P102-02 did-not-fire; the weekend exchange was US-Iran); no coalition resignation (P102-03 unfired). Lebanon moved from a talks track to a SIGNED framework (state-level de-escalation), with Hezbollah rejection keeping the spoiler latent
finding_cards.direction: down (Lebanon-axis accident risk at the state level; Hezbollah-rejection spoiler latent)
finding_cards.proposed_revision: No standalone matrix move; the Lebanon framework signing supports the Fork C de-escalation on Surface 2 (offsetting part of the Surface-1 re-arm). BS-3 state note: Israel-Lebanon framework signed June 26 (withdrawal tied to Hezbollah disarmament; no mandated Israeli pullout); Hezbollah rejects ('null and void'). The Powell pre-emption incentive stayed UNSPENT on Iran even during an active US-Iran exchange -- the strongest T8 non-spend datapoint
reference_trends: T8 hold/easing -- pre-emption incentive maximal but unspent on Iran even during the US-Iran exchange; Lebanon to a signed framework
T4 hold -- Ben Gvir/Smotrich reject the Iran deal; deal-faction/ceasefire hold
finding_cards.conflict_notes: A2 (Netanyahu Penetration-relay) is INHERITED per v4.5 (demoted; window closed). This cycle is further evidence-against any penetrated-executive-relaying-maximalism read: the US struck Iran on its OWN self-defense framing (shipping), not Israeli maximalist terms, kept the strikes infrastructure-scoped, and brokered a Lebanon framework that Israel signed but Hezbollah rejected. The diplomatic-spoiler A23 (Lebanon axis) is now at a signed framework -- the Hezbollah 'null and void' rejection is the residual spoiler vector, not an Israeli bolt-from-the-blue on Iran's nuclear sites.

finding_cards.target: Gulf state troika / unaligned-middle pivot capacity (BS-18) -- brake durability, intra-troika decay, Gulf exposure, Iran-Gulf relations
sources_consulted: Al Jazeera ('Iran attacks Kuwait and Bahrain', June 28)
PBS NewsHour (Gulf reactions)
Bahrain/Kuwait/UAE/Jordan official statements (named, via reporting)
CNN (June 24, prior Gulf 'disastrous turning point' alarm, carried)
discounts_applied: Official Gulf statements T1-direct (government condemnations)
IRGC base-damage claim excluded
finding_cards.finding: Iran struck GULF SOIL -- the IRGC's missile/drone operation hit US bases inside Kuwait (Ali Al Salem AB) and Bahrain (5th Fleet HQ), drawing UNIFIED Gulf+ condemnation of IRAN: Bahrain ('violated its sovereignty,' undermined de-escalation), Kuwait ('repeated heinous Iranian aggressions'), the UAE ('blatant violation' of sovereignty, 'threat to security and stability'), and Jordan ('dangerous escalation... blatant breach of international law'). This is a sharp shift from the Day-119 'relief->alarm at the DEAL's terms' posture to direct condemnation of Iranian aggression on Gulf territory. The condemnation is CONVERGENT across the troika (UAE aligned with Bahrain/Kuwait) -- NO named MBS-MBZ split. The Gulf exercised autonomous voice against Iran, distinct from its brake-against-US/Israeli-strikes function: Iran spent deterrent capital against its own host-states, hardening the Iran-Gulf disposition while the Gulf-US alignment held.
finding_cards.trigger_match: yes (disposition datapoint) -- Iran attacked Gulf soil; unified Gulf+Jordan condemnation of Iran. The brake/endorsement function holds (no named intra-troika split); the Iran-Gulf relationship hardens sharply. Not a Gulf-Iran war (condemnation, not military response)
finding_cards.direction: no change (matrix); Gulf disposition toward Iran hardens; Gulf-US alignment holds; no named MBS-MBZ split
finding_cards.proposed_revision: None; T1 holds (autonomous Gulf pivot capacity, this cycle pointed AGAINST Iranian aggression). Update the intra-troika asymmetric-decay candidate: UAE condemnation is CONVERGENT with Bahrain/Kuwait (aligned against Iran on the base strikes), a third opposite-signed datapoint on the MBZ pathway across recent cycles (D115 Hormuz-hawkish; D119 asset-unlock accommodation; D123 condemnation of Iran's Gulf strikes) -- pathway complexity, still no named MBS-MBZ split. Promote only on a named split or a Gulf-infrastructure attack test
reference_trends: T1 hold -- Gulf autonomous pivot capacity; unified condemnation of Iran for striking Gulf soil; no named intra-troika split; Gulf-US alignment holds
finding_cards.conflict_notes: The Gulf condemnation is a different vector from the modeled brake (which restrains US/Israeli strikes ON Iran). Here Iran attacked Gulf-hosted US bases and the unaligned middle exercised autonomous voice AGAINST Iran -- consistent with T1 (autonomous pivot capacity, direction-agnostic), not a contradiction. No principal-validation issue (named government statements). The intra-troika candidate now carries three datapoints of opposite sign on the UAE/MBZ pathway, reinforcing complexity-not-split.

finding_cards.target: Mutual dispositional reading / power-shift perception (BS-14) -- Trump-Iran, Trump-Netanyahu, Powell mechanism
sources_consulted: ABC/Fox (Trump 'Iran will no longer exist' / 'complete the job')
NPR (US/Iran retaliatory strikes)
Al Jazeera (Iran 'ready to respond'; threatens to halt talks)
Times of Israel (Ben Gvir/Smotrich 'not bound')
discounts_applied: Trump maximalist rhetoric scored near-zero; the strike order is the data
finding_cards.finding: The Trump-Iran dispositional axis went ACUTE then de-escalated this cycle -- a reserved option WAS exercised kinetically by both sides (unlike D119, where no reserved option was spent). Trump read the Ever Lovely attack as a casus belli and exercised the US strike option; Iran exercised its base-strike retaliation option; both then read the cascade risk and selected the off-ramp (cease + Doha). The Powell mechanism operated visibly: each actor read its window, exercised a reserved option to demonstrate resolve, then capped it. Trump-Netanyahu/Israel: the US brokered a Lebanon framework Israel signed (Ben Gvir/Smotrich dissent), de-escalating that axis. The dispositional divergence is real and was ACTED ON this cycle (kinetic exercise), then de-escalated -- acute-then-reset, not standing-latent as at D119.
finding_cards.trigger_match: partial -- dispositional divergence went acute (kinetic exercise of reserved options on the US-Iran axis) then de-escalated; below a fork-level matrix move (no US KIA, no Fork A conversion). Feeds the Fork C realization (PROBE-8/16)
finding_cards.direction: no change (matrix); the divergence was exercised kinetically then reset
finding_cards.proposed_revision: No standalone matrix move; folds into the Fork C re-arm. BS-14 state note: a reserved option was kinetically exercised on the US-Iran axis this cycle (US strikes + Iran base strikes) and then capped -- the Powell read-your-window mechanism use-confirmed, with both actors selecting the off-ramp once the cascade risk read as mutually unaffordable
reference_trends: T8 hold -- Powell loop exercised kinetically this cycle then capped (incentive predicted; actor selected demonstration-then-restraint)
T4 hold -- coalition not monolithic (Ben Gvir/Smotrich reject; deal-faction holds; Lebanon framework signed)
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Consistent with T8 (incentive predicted; actor selects -- and selected demonstration-then-de-escalation) and T4 (coalition not monolithic). No contradiction. The Trump-Iran coercive axis is event-contingent and went acute on the vessel attacks, then de-elevated on the de-escalation agreement; the structural divergence stands.

finding_cards.target: US domestic war-powers / constitutional constraint (BS-5) -- WPR votes, court WPA filings, executive-path lock-in (T9)
sources_consulted: CBS ('Senate rejects measure to restrict Trump's Iran war powers as key Republicans shift votes', 50-47, June 24)
CNN (Senate 50-48, June 23, carried)
NPR (June 23 symbolic vote)
NPR ('Trump says US and Iran will meet in Qatar', June 29)
discounts_applied: No -50% needed; named roll-call votes, multi-outlet
finding_cards.finding: No NEW war-powers vote this cycle; two reinforcing datapoints for the T9-CONTESTED-but-executive-path-holds reading. (1) Backfill: one day after the 50-48 passage, the Senate REJECTED a measure to restrict Trump's Iran war powers (procedural motion failed 50-47; only 2 Republicans for) -- an on-merits countervailing datapoint not captured at D119. (2) Decisive: Trump ORDERED CENTCOM strikes on five Iranian sites June 26-27, days after both chambers passed the WPR and he dismissed it ('I will get it done, one way or the other') -- the executive struck Iran with ZERO enforceable constraint, the operational lock-in holding in fact. No court granted WPA standing. T9 holds CONTESTED: the bicameral on-merits passages stand (not back to VALIDATED), but the executive path is operational (not DISCONFIRMED).
finding_cards.trigger_match: partial -- no new WPR trigger; the cycle REINFORCES T9 CONTESTED-not-DISCONFIRMED (executive struck Iran free of constraint; June-24 procedural rejection countervails). No state change. PROBE-10 holds Tier D (auto-upshift from the D119 fire runs through 3 cycles)
finding_cards.direction: no change (T9 holds CONTESTED; executive-path prediction reinforced)
finding_cards.proposed_revision: BS-5 state note: the executive struck Iran days after both chambers passed the WPR and dismissed it, with the June-24 Senate procedural rejection (50-47) countervailing -- the strongest in-fact demonstration that the executive-path lock-in operationally holds under T9 CONTESTED. Standing watch unchanged: a Federal court granting WPA standing OR a veto-override-capable majority crosses toward DISCONFIRMED
reference_trends: T9 hold -- CONTESTED; executive path operationally holds (struck Iran post-WPR; June-24 procedural rejection countervails)
finding_cards.conflict_notes: No principal-validation issue (named roll-call votes; on-tape strike order). The internal tension is resolved in the executive-path direction this cycle: the most direct possible test (did the WPR constrain a strike decision?) ran -- Trump struck Iran days after the WPR -- and the lock-in held in fact. CONTESTED remains correct (the bicameral on-merits passages are real instrumentation), not a reversion to VALIDATED and not DISCONFIRMED.

finding_cards.target: Iranian apex / decision architecture (BS-1a) -- Mojtaba/Pezeshkian/SNSC, MoU ownership, apex-direct discriminator
sources_consulted: NPR/PBS (strikes IRGC-attributed; Iran threatens to halt talks)
Al Jazeera (Gharibabadi: technical talks 'when conditions met')
Times of Israel/NBC (Mojtaba unseen since Feb 28, carried)
Iran International (hardliner backlash, T2 exile -50%)
discounts_applied: Iran International -50% (exile/opposition)
Single-source Iranian internal-politics -70% where uncorroborated
No authenticated apex-direct statement surfaced; the June-18 message remains an attributed relay
finding_cards.finding: No authenticated apex-direct statement this cycle, and no resurrection signal for the structurally-opaque A4 read (demoted at v4.5). The base strikes on Kuwait/Bahrain were IRGC-attributed (naval+aerospace) with NO apex/SNSC ownership claim; the immediate cease-and-resume-Doha agreement points to council-level control of the off-ramp -- consistent with a diffuse/delegated council that consents to the deal while the military brandishes. The deputy-FM track (Gharibabadi) carried the public Iranian voice on both the verification deferral and the talks-hedging ('when conditions met'). Mojtaba remains unseen/unheard since Feb 28; P115-03 (authenticated apex-direct) unmet; P84-07 (Vahidi-direct HEU) absent (12th). The structurally-opaque read is reinforced, not challenged.
finding_cards.trigger_match: partial -- no apex-ownership signal; no authenticated apex-direct; A4 resurrection condition unmet. The strikes-without-apex-claim + council-controlled off-ramp reinforce the v4.5 structurally-opaque read. Below matrix-movement threshold
finding_cards.direction: no change (matrix); structurally-opaque A4 reinforced (apex-unowned strikes; council-controlled off-ramp)
finding_cards.proposed_revision: BS-1a state note: the base strikes were IRGC-attributed and apex-unowned, the off-ramp council-controlled -- consistent with the v4.5 structurally-opaque demotion (diffuse/delegated council consents without a legible owner). No resurrection of a named-decider attribution; any future attribution enters at provisional confidence
reference_trends: T3 hold -- two-level structure holds (military brandishes/exercises the floor; diffuse council consents to the deal; apex unowned)
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Principal-validation (A4 STRUCTURALLY-OPAQUE per v4.5; the provisional window CLOSED at v4.5, so no provisional forcing-question is owed). Resurrection condition (an authenticated apex-direct term-level statement owning or repudiating a specific MoU term, or apex/SNSC public ownership of the base strikes) did NOT fire this cycle -- all evidence remains on the delegated/diffuse side (Mojtaba unseen; strikes IRGC-attributed and apex-unowned; off-ramp council-controlled). The structurally-opaque read is the operative one; no re-narrowing to a named decider is warranted.

finding_cards.target: Iranian residual capability / feigned weakness (BS-13) -- reconstitution rate, T12 amplifier
sources_consulted: Standing US-IC / Israeli-IC baseline (not re-sourced this window)
JPost (two CENTCOM targets newly-built by Iran, June 27)
discounts_applied: No fresh reconstitution datapoint in the June-26-29 window; standing baseline reaffirmed, not re-sourced (-confidence)
finding_cards.finding: No new reconstitution-rate cluster this cycle. The standing US-IC/Israeli-IC baseline carries (faster-than-IC timelines; ~half missile launchers intact; full drone reconstitution ~6 months; ~440kg 60% HEU survived). One adjacent datapoint: JPost reports two of the CENTCOM weekend targets were NEWLY-BUILT Iranian sites -- consistent with (not a fresh measure of) ongoing reconstruction. Iran's demonstrated capability to mount a coordinated naval+aerospace ballistic+drone strike on two Gulf states is residual-capability USE-confirmation, but not a new reconstitution-rate assessment. Capability frozen-pending-verification at the table.
finding_cards.trigger_match: no -- T12 holds; no fresh reconstitution cluster (the newly-built-targets and the base-strike capability are use-confirmation, not a new rate assessment)
finding_cards.proposed_revision: None; T12 VALIDATED holds on the prior clusters. Note the newly-built-targets datapoint (JPost) and the demonstrated two-country strike capability as use-confirmation for /audit (BS-13), not a matrix mover
reference_trends: T12 hold -- amplifier real; capability use-confirmed (two-country strike) but no fresh reconstitution-rate cluster
finding_cards.conflict_notes: The reconstitution amplifier (T12) and the Israeli pre-emption incentive (T8) both remain loaded; the actor channeled none of it into an Iran-nuclear strike even during an active US-Iran exchange, consistent with both trends (incentive predicted; actor selects restraint).

sources_consulted: NPR/Al Jazeera (US strikes / Iran base strikes / de-escalation)
ABC/Fox (Trump 'Iran will no longer exist' / 'complete the job')
Fox (Iran agrees to halt attacks; talks loom)
discounts_applied: Trump 'regime could cease to exist' rhetoric scored near-zero (coercive bargaining, not a death-ground inversion)
finding_cards.finding: The CLOSEST approach to the death-ground fire-bar of the conflict (an actual bilateral strike exchange) -- but still NO death-ground dynamic. No US targeting of named Iranian principals (P-DG unfired; strikes hit infrastructure -- radar, drone storage, mine-laying -- not principals). Iranian terms did NOT harden into death-ground maximalism: Iran threatened to halt talks then agreed (tentatively) to cease and resume Doha -- bargaining, not last-stand. Trump's 'Iran will no longer exist' / 'complete the job' is coercive bargaining rhetoric that did NOT convert to principal-targeting or unlimited aims (P-AIM: US aims stayed limited/self-defense-of-shipping; Iranian aims stayed bargaining). No P-INFO purge/sycophant signal; the hardliner contestation is managed-ratification friction, not an information-pathology purge. P-DG2 (post-strike demand-hardening): Iran threatened then de-escalated -- the inverse of hardening.
finding_cards.trigger_match: no -- the partials/0-fires pattern continues even through a live strike exchange: no principal targeting, no unlimited aims, off-ramp taken. The fire-bar held structurally unreachable absent a sustained open-resumption phase
finding_cards.proposed_revision: None on the matrix. The fired-bar recalibration remains a next-/audit item, now with a STRONGER datapoint: even an actual bilateral US-Iran strike exchange produced a 0-fire (no principal targeting, off-ramp taken), confirming the bar is reserved for a death-ground/decapitation event in a sustained kinetic-resumption phase, not a bounded exchange-then-de-escalation. Carry the redefinition to /audit
reference_trends: T3 hold -- apex-deterrent floor with a signed off-ramp; bounded exchange then de-escalation, not a death-ground inversion
finding_cards.conflict_notes: No principal-targeting; A20 PA-gap death-ground risk dormant. The closest death-ground-adjacent signals (Trump 'complete the job'; the actual strikes) are bounded coercion that did not convert to principal targeting or unlimited aims. The probe's persistent 0-fire even through a live exchange is a fire-bar-definition question for /audit, not a world-state gap. The v4.5 A4 structurally-opaque demotion sharpens the latent death-ground risk (decapitating a diffuse council with no legible owner may not resolve the PA-gap), but no targeting fired this cycle.

trigger_digest.trigger: The shipping/Hormuz accident surface RE-ARMED and FIRED into a real bilateral US-Iran kinetic exchange (June 26-28), one cycle after the D119 'declaratory/unenforced de-arm.' Iranian drone attacks on two commercial vessels (Ever Lovely, Singapore; Kiku, Panama) -> CENTCOM strikes on FIVE Iranian coastal/military sites (self-defense-of-shipping framing, NO operation name, infrastructure-scoped) -> IRGC naval+aerospace ballistic+drone strikes on US bases in Kuwait (Ali Al Salem) and Bahrain (5th Fleet HQ), claiming '8 bases destroyed' -> CENTCOM/US-official report ZERO damage and ZERO US KIA -> mutual cease + Doha-resumption agreement (June 30; Iran unconfirmed). Brent ticked up ~0.9% to ~$73.21 (war premium nearly fully unwound).
trigger_digest.proposed_action: 30d matrix: Fork C 14-22 -> 18-28 (re-arm/realization); Fork D'-i 30-42 -> 26-38 (window non-clean; Iran hedging Doha); Fork D' composite 50-64 -> 48-62; KEC 24-42 -> 28-48 (footnote). Fork A composite HOLD 10-18 (cascade-to-A barrier held under live fire: zero KIA, no operation name, de-escalation). Resolve P108-04 (Brent>$92) / P105-05 (>$100) / P105-01 (new operation) / P93-04 (US KIA) did-not-fire. P115-02 partial (Iran fired on commercial third-flag shipping AND US bases, but NOT US/allied warships, and zero US KIA -> Fork C not Fork A). ESCALATE to /audit + /premortem: the BS-7/BS-11 Hormuz sub-read has now oscillated FOUR consecutive cycles (D110 settled -> D115 re-arm -> D119 declaratory -> D123 live-exchange); the Day-119 sweep's own 'is the sub-read tracking each headline?' watch is CONFIRMED. Recommend a standing Fork-C floor so the live surface is not zeroed on quiet cycles. No /revise: within-architecture §5.28/§5.30 activation.

trigger_digest.trigger: A1 (Trump) CONVERTED rhetoric to bounded tape action -- ordered CENTCOM strikes on five Iranian sites (June 26-27) after citing the Ever Lovely attack as a ceasefire violation -- while keeping the action DEAL-PROTECTIVE (infrastructure-scoped, no principal/nuclear targeting, immediate Doha re-opening). Vance: 'violence will be met with violence.' Trump maximalist rhetoric ('Iran will no longer exist', 'complete the job') discounted near-zero; the bounded strike is the data.
trigger_digest.proposed_action: No fork-level move (Fork A holds 10-18; US-resumption sub-path ticks up within band, offset by Israeli sub-path easing on the Lebanon framework). BS-12 state note: A1 use-confirmed the 'hawkish-with-bounded-execution, deal-protective' sub-state (struck, then re-opened talks). Watch: a next strike naming an operation / hitting a principal or nuclear site / producing US KIA converts to a Fork A trigger.

trigger_digest.trigger: The signed deferral SURVIVED a live bilateral kinetic exchange (talks set to resume Doha June 30; signature intact) but Iran is HEDGING attendance (Gharibabadi: technical talks 'not yet planned... when conditions are met'). The §5.30 verification fracture FIRMS: IAEA DG Grossi asserts the MoU 'explicitly' mandates IAEA supervision and inspections will happen; Iran defers access to 'a final agreement' (P109-03 continues, now with Grossi placing the text on the supervision side).
trigger_digest.proposed_action: Log P109-03 continues/firms. 30d matrix: Fork D'-i 30-42 -> 26-38 (Iran Doha-hedging; window non-clean); Fork D'-v hold 22-30 (leading variant; verification fracture firms but instrument holds). BS-11 state note: deferral absorbed the strike exchange and held; verification (IAEA access timing) is the central disputed term. Discriminating signal next cycle: (a) does Iran actually appear at Doha June 30, (b) an agreed/published IAEA-access protocol vs sustained Iranian deferral hardening to deal-terminal.

principal_validation_window.note: A4 and A2 provisional windows CLOSED at v4.5 (June 25 /revise consumed the demotion manifest). No provisional forcing-question is owed this cycle; the cards record only whether a resurrection signal fired.

All evidence remains on the delegated/diffuse side. The structurally-opaque read is reinforced; any new attribution enters at provisional confidence.

The US struck Iran on its own self-defense-of-shipping framing (not Israeli maximalist terms), kept the strikes infrastructure-scoped, and brokered a Lebanon framework Israel signed but Hezbollah rejected. The diplomatic-spoiler A23 (Lebanon axis) is now at a signed framework; the residual spoiler vector is the Hezbollah 'null and void' rejection, not an Israeli strike on Iran's nuclear sites.
